content.problemStatement:
You are given a string sss consisting of English letters (uppercase and lowercase), digits, and spaces `' '`. The string may contain leading or trailing spaces, or multiple spaces between words.

Your task is to **reverse the order of the words** in the string. A word is defined as a sequence of non-space characters.

The resulting string should:


Contain words in reversed order.
Have only **single spaces** separating words.
Not contain leading or trailing spaces.
Function DeclarationFunction NamereverseWordsreverseWordsreverseWords – This function reverses the order of words in a given string while ensuring that words are separated by exactly one space and there are no leading or trailing spaces.

Parameters
sss : A string consisting of English letters (uppercase and lowercase), digits, and spaces `' '`.
Return Value
Returns a string containing the words of sss in reversed order.
The returned string:

Contains words separated by a single space.
Has no leading or trailing spaces.


Constraints
1≤∣s∣≤1041 \le |s| \le 10^41≤∣s∣≤104
The string sss contains:

Uppercase English letters (A–ZA–ZA–Z)
Lowercase English letters (a–za–za–z)
Digits (0–90–90–9)
Space characters (`' '`)


The string may contain:

Leading spaces
Trailing spaces
Multiple consecutive spaces between words


There is **at least one word** in sss.

Input Format

The first line contains a single string sss.


Output Format

Print a single line containing the words of sss in reversed order.
Words must be separated by exactly one space.
There must be no leading or trailing spaces in the output.
constraints: s contains only uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits and space characters
Each s contains at least one word
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string s consisting of English letters (uppercase and lowercase), digits, and spaces. The string may have leading or trailing spaces and multiple consecutive spaces. There is at least one word in each s.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the words of s in reversed order. Words must be separated by exactly one space with no leading or trailing spaces.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
OpenAI   creates amazing   AI   models
Data   Science is fun
Hello World
sampleTestCases.output:
models AI amazing creates OpenAI
fun is Science Data
World Hello
sampleTestCases.explanation: Each line is split into words, the order of the words is reversed, and the words are joined with a single space.
Leading/trailing/multiple spaces are ignored when extracting words. After reversal the words are printed with exactly one space.
The three words are reversed; case of letters is preserved.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
1
alpha beta   gamma delta    epsilon zeta eta theta iota kappa
hidden.expectedOutput: kappa iota theta eta zeta epsilon delta gamma beta alpha

hidden.label: edge case with numbers and spaces

solution.approach: For each test case we need to extract the words, ignoring any number of spaces. The easiest way is to split the line on whitespace. In C/C++/Java we can use strtok, stringstream or regex split; in Python and JavaScript the built‑in split() without arguments already discards empty tokens. After obtaining a vector/list of words we output them in reverse order, joining with a single space. This runs in linear time with respect to the length of the input string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all input strings) – each character is examined a constant number of times.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(total length of all input strings) – we store the words of the current test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0].strip())
    out = []
    for i in range(1, T + 1):
        line = data[i]
        words = line.split()  # split on any whitespace, discarding empties
        out.append(' '.join(reversed(words)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
